docs.title: Sampler Lab
docs.text:
Sampler Lab is a from-scratch Monte Carlo methods library and experiment suite. The project emphasizes transparent algorithms, explicit random-number state, stable log-domain calculations, and mathematical validation.

The fixed-seed quick benchmark deliberately exposes funnel-neck error and other method-specific failure modes. See the reference benchmark summary for the full comparison.

docs.title: What is implemented
docs.text:
• exact transforms and rejection sampling;
  • importance sampling and rare-event twisting;
  • sequential importance sampling, resampling, and particle ancestry;
  • finite-state Markov theory and exact asymptotic variance;
  • Metropolis--Hastings, Gibbs, Ising, Langevin, HMC, and underdamped methods;
  • annealed importance sampling and annealed SMC;
  • conditioning, stochastic Newton, and affine-invariant ensembles;
  • adaptive MCMC and policy-gradient proposal learning;
  • variational and Stein methods with explicit exact-versus-approximate labels;
  • a capability-aware cross-method benchmark on Gaussian mixtures and funnel targets.

See the development roadmap, generated API reference, public API policy, and public references.

docs.title: Design principles
docs.text:
1. Every stochastic API receives an explicit numpy.random.Generator.
  2. Log densities and log weights are the default numerical representation.
  3. Rejections, resampling, adaptation, and training costs remain visible in returned diagnostics.
  4. Exact, corrected, weighted, and approximate outputs are never conflated.
  5. Deterministic identities and invariance tests precede statistical regression tests.
  6. Benchmarks report capabilities and exclusions rather than inventing a universal winner score.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
Sampler Lab is not currently published on PyPI. Install the current source directly:

python -m pip install "git+https://github.com/TylerPerlman/sampler-lab.git"

For an editable development checkout:

git clone https://github.com/TylerPerlman/sampler-lab.git
cd sampler-lab
python -m pip install -e '.[dev]'
ruff check .
ruff format --check .
mypy src/sampler_lab
pytest -m "not statistical"
mkdocs build --strict

docs.title: Repository health
docs.text: GitHub Actions validates formatting, linting, strict typing, compilation, package installation, PEP 561 metadata, console entry points, executable documentation examples, unit coverage, isolated statistical tests, documentation, and release builds. A scheduled weekly run checks the current supported dependency set for numerical drift. GitHub default setup provides CodeQL scanning when the repository is eligible, and Dependabot monitors dependencies.

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page is generated from the installed package's public namespace exports, docstrings, and type annotations. Package-level exports are the supported discovery surface; deep modules remain useful for implementation study but are not automatically stable.

docs.title: sampler_lab
docs.text: Transparent Monte Carlo sampling methods implemented from scratch.

docs.title: OperationCounter dataclass
docs.text:
Mutable counts of stochastic and expensive deterministic operations.

The named fields cover the common cost units used across the package. extra permits algorithm-specific counts without immediately changing the common interface.

docs.title: increment(name, amount=1)
docs.text: Increment a named operation by a nonnegative integer amount.

docs.title: merge(other)
docs.text: Add another counter into this one and return self.

docs.title: snapshot()
docs.text: Return a detached dictionary suitable for result diagnostics.

docs.title: make_rng(seed=None)
docs.text: Create a NumPy generator without touching global random state.

docs.title: spawn_rngs(seed, n_streams)
docs.text: Create reproducible, statistically independent child streams.

docs.title: Parameters
docs.text: seed: Entropy used for the parent SeedSequence. n_streams: Number of child generators. Must be nonnegative.

docs.title: sampler_lab.core
docs.text: Core components for sampler-lab.

docs.title: sampler_lab.estimators
docs.text: Monte Carlo estimators and error summaries.

docs.title: ErrorMetrics dataclass
docs.text: Empirical error decomposition for replicated scalar estimates.

docs.title: iid_estimate(samples, observable=None)
docs.text:
Estimate a scalar expectation from IID samples.

observable is evaluated once on the full sample array and should return one scalar value per sample. When omitted, samples itself must be one-dimensional. The reported variance is the unbiased sample variance for n >= 2.

docs.title: estimate_normalization_ratio(log_weights)
docs.text:
Estimate a normalizing-constant ratio from importance log weights.

If X ~ q and log_weights = log(tilde_p(X)) - log(q(X)), this estimates Z_p. More generally, when samples are drawn from the normalized version of tilde_q, the same calculation estimates Z_p / Z_q.

The logarithm is always computed stably. The linear estimate is inf only when the result is too large to represent in float64.

docs.title: weighted_mean(samples, normalized_weights, observable=None)
docs.text: Compute a scalar weighted mean using normalized weights.

docs.title: weighted_variance(samples, normalized_weights, observable=None, *, unbiased=False)
docs.text:
Compute weighted variance about the weighted mean.

With unbiased=True, the reliability-weight correction 1 / (1 - sum(weights**2)) is applied. It is undefined when a single observation carries all weight.

docs.title: sampler_lab.exact
docs.text: Exact sampling algorithms.

docs.title: box_muller(rng, size, *, counter=None)
docs.text:
Generate standard normal variates with the Box-Muller transform.

For odd size, one member of the last generated pair is intentionally discarded. Operation counts record the uniforms actually consumed and normals returned.

docs.title: generalized_inverse_discrete(rng, values, probabilities, size, *, counter=None)
docs.text:
Sample a finite distribution using its right-continuous generalized inverse.

values may be any finite numeric support. Probabilities are normalized after validation, avoiding a false precision requirement that they sum to exactly one.

docs.title: inverse_cdf_sample(rng, inverse_cdf, size, *, counter=None)
docs.text: Sample by applying an inverse CDF to independent uniforms on [0, 1).

docs.title: rejection_sample(rng, size, proposal_sampler, log_target, log_proposal, log_envelope, *, batch_size=1024, max_proposals=None, counter=None, envelope_tolerance=1e-12)
docs.text:
Draw from a target using a dominating proposal.

The caller supplies log_envelope = log(M) such that target(x) <= M * proposal(x) for every proposal-reachable x. The target may be unnormalized provided M uses the same normalization convention.

Density callables receive one proposal at a time. Proposal generation is batched so model implementations can still use vectorized random-number generation.

docs.title: polar_to_cartesian(radius, angle)
docs.text: Convert broadcast-compatible polar coordinates to (..., 2) Cartesian points.

docs.title: sampler_lab.importance
docs.text: Importance sampling estimators, diagnostics, and controlled experiments.

docs.title: GaussianTailRow dataclass
docs.text: One proposal's estimate of a standard-normal upper-tail probability.

docs.title: GaussianWeightCollapseRow dataclass
docs.text: Empirical and exact diagnostics for one product-space dimension.

docs.title: chi_squared_divergence_estimate(log_weights)
docs.text:
Estimate Pearson chi^2(p || q) up to weight normalization.

The scale-invariant plug-in estimate is mean((w / mean(w) - 1)**2) = n * sum(omega**2) - 1. It is therefore also the empirical squared coefficient of variation of the raw weights.

docs.title: gaussian_scale_chi_squared(dimension, *, target_scale=1.0, proposal_scale=1.0)
docs.text:
Return exact chi^2(p || q) for centered isotropic Gaussians.

p = N(0, target_scale^2 I) and q = N(0, proposal_scale^2 I). The divergence is infinite when the proposal tails are too light: 2 proposal_scale^2 <= target_scale^2.

docs.title: product_chi_squared(single_factor_chi_squared, dimension)
docs.text: Lift a one-factor chi-squared divergence to an IID product target.

docs.title: renyi_divergence_order_two_estimate(log_weights)
docs.text: Estimate order-two Renyi divergence as log(1 + chi^2).

docs.title: self_normalized_importance_estimate(samples, log_weights, observable=None)
docs.text:
Estimate a target expectation from unnormalized importance weights.

The standard error uses the plug-in delta-method variance

n/(n-1) * sum_i omega_i^2 (f_i - estimate)^2.

The reported bias approximation is the usual second-order ratio-estimator approximation and is nan for a single observation.

docs.title: standard_importance_estimate(samples, log_weights, observable=None)
docs.text:
Estimate E_q[w(X) f(X)] when the target normalization is known.

log_weights should be log p(X) - log q(X) for normalized p and q. The standard error is the IID sample standard deviation of the weighted contributions divided by sqrt(n).

docs.title: gaussian_tail_experiment(rng, threshold, n_samples, proposal_means)
docs.text:
Compare shifted-normal proposals for a standard-normal upper tail.

For q = N(mu, 1) and p = N(0, 1), the exact log weight is -mu * x + mu**2 / 2. mu=0 is ordinary crude Monte Carlo.

docs.title: standard_normal_upper_tail(threshold)
docs.text: Return P(Z >= threshold) for Z ~ N(0, 1).

docs.title: gaussian_product_log_weights(samples, *, target_scale=1.0, proposal_scale=1.0)
docs.text: Compute log p - log q for centered isotropic Gaussian arrays.

docs.title: gaussian_weight_collapse_experiment(rng, dimensions, n_samples, *, target_scale=1.0, proposal_scale=1.25)
docs.text: Measure importance-weight collapse as product dimension grows.

docs.title: log_importance_weights(samples, log_target, log_proposal, *, counter=None)
docs.text:
Evaluate log_target - log_proposal for each sampled observation.

The first array axis indexes observations. A finite target density where the proposal density is zero is rejected as a support violation rather than represented by a misleading infinite weight.

docs.title: sampler_lab.particles
docs.text: Sequential importance sampling and particle resampling.

docs.title: Ancestry dataclass
docs.text:
Parent maps between consecutive particle populations.

parent_indices[t][j] is the index at time t of particle j at time t + 1. Population sizes may vary, as under Bernoulli resampling.

docs.title: n_steps property
docs.text: Number of parent-child transitions.

docs.title: trace_lineage(final_index)
docs.text: Return one lineage from generation zero through the final generation.

docs.title: final_to_initial()
docs.text: Map every final particle to its generation-zero ancestor.

docs.title: unique_ancestor_counts()
docs.text: Count distinct ancestors of the final population at every generation.

docs.title: ParticleCloud dataclass
docs.text:
Particles paired with normalized log weights.

The particle axis is always the first axis. Inputs are copied and marked read-only so a cloud is a stable snapshot suitable for history tracking. Additive shifts in the supplied log weights are discarded by normalization.

docs.title: n_particles property
docs.text: Current population size.

docs.title: weights property
docs.text: Normalized linear weights as a fresh array.

docs.title: diagnostics property
docs.text: Scale-free diagnostics for the current weights.

docs.title: effective_sample_size property
docs.text: Kish effective sample size of the weights.

docs.title: uniform(particles) classmethod
docs.text: Construct a uniformly weighted cloud.

docs.title: expectation(observable)
docs.text: Evaluate a scalar- or array-valued weighted empirical expectation.

docs.title: select(parent_indices)
docs.text: Copy selected parents and reset their weights to uniform.

docs.title: BernoulliResampler dataclass
docs.text:
Independent floor-plus-Bernoulli offspring counts.

The expected population size is n_particles but the realized size is random. Each expected offspring count is split into its integer floor and an independent Bernoulli draw for the fractional remainder, so the expected count is unchanged. An empty population is possible and is deliberately not retried.

docs.title: MultinomialResampler dataclass
docs.text: Independent categorical parent draws with fixed population size.

docs.title: ResamplingDiagnostics dataclass
docs.text: Observed offspring allocation from one resampling operation.

docs.title: SystematicResampler dataclass
docs.text: One-random-number fixed-population systematic resampling.

docs.title: ParticleExtinctionError
docs.text:
Bases: RuntimeError

Raised when every particle has zero weight or resampling returns none.

docs.title: PropagationResult dataclass
docs.text: Proposed particles and their incremental log weights.

docs.title: SequentialImportanceResult dataclass
docs.text: Complete history of a sequential importance-sampling run.

docs.title: n_steps property
docs.text: Number of sequential propagation steps.

docs.title: final_cloud property
docs.text: Final post-resampling cloud.

docs.title: final_weighted_cloud property
docs.text: Final target-weighted cloud before any last resampling operation.

docs.title: normalizing_constant_estimate property
docs.text: Normalizing-constant estimate in linear space.

docs.title: ess_history property
docs.text: Pre-resampling effective sample size after each propagation.

docs.title: population_sizes property
docs.text: Post-resampling population size at each generation.

docs.title: SequentialProposal
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

One sequential proposal-and-weighting operation.

docs.title: propose(particles, step, rng)
docs.text: Extend every particle and return incremental log weights.

docs.title: minimal_conditional_variances(weights, n_particles)
docs.text: Minimum possible marginal offspring variances for unbiased integer counts.

docs.title: multinomial_conditional_variances(weights, n_particles)
docs.text: Return Var[N_i | weights] for multinomial resampling.

docs.title: offspring_counts(parent_indices, n_parents)
docs.text: Count offspring assigned to each parent.

docs.title: resampling_diagnostics(parent_indices, n_parents)
docs.text: Summarize an observed parent-index vector.

docs.title: sequential_importance_sampling(initial_particles, n_steps, proposal, rng, *, initial_log_weights=None, resampler=None, resample_every_step=False, resample_ess_fraction=None, target_particle_count=None, initial_log_normalizing_constant=0.0)
docs.text:
Run sequential importance sampling with optional resampling.

At each step the algorithm propagates, applies incremental weights, updates the normalizing-constant estimate, and then optionally resamples. resample_ess_fraction triggers when pre-resampling ESS is at most that fraction of the current population. A supplied target_particle_count is useful for variable-population rules such as Bernoulli resampling.

docs.title: sampler_lab.markov
docs.text: Finite-state Markov operators, structure, and exact error analysis.

docs.title: CommunicationStructure dataclass
docs.text: Communicating classes and recurrent closed classes of a finite chain.

docs.title: FiniteStateMarkovChain dataclass
docs.text:
A row-stochastic transition matrix with exact structural operations.

Functions are column vectors and measures are row vectors. Consequently P @ f applies the transition operator while mu @ P evolves a distribution. The matrix is copied and made read-only at construction.

docs.title: generator property
docs.text: Discrete generator L = P - I used throughout the package.

docs.title: period property
docs.text: Return the common state period of an irreducible chain.

docs.title: is_aperiodic property
docs.text: Whether every closed communicating class has period one.

docs.title: is_ergodic property
docs.text: Use the standard finite-state meaning: irreducible and aperiodic.

docs.title: apply(observable, *, steps=1)
docs.text: Apply P**steps to an observable.

docs.title: pushforward(measure, *, steps=1)
docs.text: Evolve a probability measure by steps transitions.

docs.title: communication_structure()
docs.text: Return all communicating classes, closed classes, and class periods.

docs.title: invariant_distributions()
docs.text:
Return one extreme invariant distribution per closed class.

Every invariant probability distribution is a convex combination of these rows. Transient states receive zero stationary mass.

docs.title: invariant_distribution()
docs.text: Return the unique invariant distribution, or reject ambiguity.

docs.title: global_balance_residual(probabilities=None)
docs.text: Return max_i |(pi P)_i - pi_i|.

docs.title: detailed_balance_residual(probabilities=None)
docs.text: Return the maximum pairwise stationary-flux imbalance.

docs.title: time_reversal(probabilities=None)
docs.text:
Construct the stationary time-reversed transition matrix.

A full-support invariant distribution is required because reverse transitions out of a zero-mass state are not identified by stationarity.

docs.title: eigenvalues()
docs.text: Return transition eigenvalues sorted by decreasing modulus.

docs.title: spectral_summary(probabilities=None)
docs.text: Compute eigenvalue, contraction, and reversible-gap diagnostics.

docs.title: constant_projection(probabilities=None)
docs.text: Return the rank-one projection 1 pi for an invariant law.

docs.title: SpectralSummary dataclass
docs.text: Exact finite-dimensional spectral diagnostics under an invariant law.

docs.title: FiniteStateTrajectory dataclass
docs.text: Immutable sequence of state indices generated by a finite-state chain.

docs.title: empirical_measure(*, discard=0)
docs.text: Return normalized state occupancies after an optional prefix discard.

docs.title: observable_values(observable)
docs.text: Evaluate a finite-state observable along the trajectory.

docs.title: ergodic_average(observable, *, discard=0)
docs.text: Return a time average after an optional prefix discard.

docs.title: MartingaleDecomposition dataclass
docs.text: Finite-path Poisson decomposition of a centered additive functional.

docs.title: PoissonEquationResult dataclass
docs.text: Centered solution of (P - I)u = f - pi[f].

docs.title: fundamental_solution property
docs.text: Return h = -u, satisfying (I - P)h = f - pi[f].

docs.title: validate_transition_matrix(transition, *, atol=1e-12)
docs.text: Validate and copy a row-stochastic transition matrix.

docs.title: sample_categorical(probabilities, rng)
docs.text: Draw one categorical index using a supplied random-number generator.

docs.title: simulate_chain(chain, rng, *, n_steps, initial_state=None, initial_distribution=None)
docs.text:
Generate n_steps transitions and retain the initial state.

Exactly one of initial_state and initial_distribution may be supplied. If neither is given, the unique invariant distribution is used.

docs.title: transition_step(chain, state, rng)
docs.text: Advance a finite-state chain by one transition.

docs.title: apply_transition(transition, observable)
docs.text: Apply a row-stochastic transition matrix to a column of function values.

docs.title: as_observable(values, *, n_states=None)
docs.text: Validate a real-valued observable on a finite state space.

docs.title: as_probability_vector(probabilities, *, n_states=None, atol=1e-12)
docs.text:
Validate, copy, and normalize a finite probability vector.

Values smaller than zero by at most atol are clipped to zero. The returned vector is always a fresh float64 array whose entries sum to one. Larger normalization errors are rejected rather than silently hidden.

docs.title: centered_observable(probabilities, observable)
docs.text: Center an observable under the supplied probability distribution.

docs.title: constant_projection(probabilities)
docs.text: Matrix mapping every observable to its constant stationary projection.

docs.title: covariance(probabilities, left, right)
docs.text: Return the covariance of two observables under one measure.

docs.title: expectation(probabilities, observable)
docs.text: Return the finite-state expectation sum_i pi_i f_i.

docs.title: operator_duality_residual(measure, transition, observable)
docs.text: Numerically check (mu P)f = mu(Pf) for row-vector conventions.

docs.title: pushforward_measure(measure, transition)
docs.text: Push a row-vector probability measure forward by one Markov step.

docs.title: variance(probabilities, observable)
docs.text: Return the stationary variance of an observable.

docs.title: weighted_inner_product(probabilities, left, right)
docs.text: Return the L2(pi) inner product of two finite-state functions.

docs.title: compose_transitions(chains, *, tolerance=1e-12)
docs.text:
Compose transitions in execution order.

compose_transitions([P, Q]) means apply P first and Q second, yielding the row-stochastic matrix P @ Q.

docs.title: conditional_component_transition(target, axis, conditional_kernels, *, tolerance=1e-12)
docs.text:
Lift conditional-invariant component kernels to the full state space.

target is an arbitrary-dimensional joint probability table. For each fixed complement of axis, conditional_kernels supplies a square transition matrix on that coordinate. Its shape must be target.shape without axis + (m, m), where m = target.shape[axis].

Each local kernel is checked for row stochasticity and preservation of the corresponding conditional target. Contexts with zero target mass use an identity transition because their behavior cannot affect invariance.

docs.title: conditional_resampling_transition(target, axis, *, tolerance=1e-12)
docs.text: Construct the exact Gibbs resampling kernel for one coordinate block.

docs.title: conjugate_by_permutation(chain, old_to_new, *, tolerance=1e-12)
docs.text:
Relabel states by a permutation, preserving all Markov properties.

old_to_new[i] is the new index assigned to old state i. The result satisfies Q[old_to_new[i], old_to_new[j]] = P[i, j].

docs.title: mixture_transition(chains, probabilities=None, *, tolerance=1e-12)
docs.text: Randomly choose one transition matrix independently of the state.

docs.title: normalize_joint_distribution(target)
docs.text: Validate and normalize a finite joint probability table.

docs.title: asymptotic_variance(chain, observable, *, probabilities=None)
docs.text:
Return the exact CLT variance of the stationary sample mean.

With L = P - I and Lu = f - pi[f], the formula is -2 <f-pi[f], u>_pi - Var_pi(f). It remains valid for irreducible periodic chains, where the naive infinite autocovariance series need not converge in the ordinary sense.

docs.title: autocorrelations(chain, observable, max_lag, *, probabilities=None)
docs.text: Return exact stationary autocorrelations through max_lag.

docs.title: autocovariances(chain, observable, max_lag, *, probabilities=None)
docs.text: Return exact stationary autocovariances from lag zero through max_lag.

docs.title: finite_sample_mean_variance(chain, observable, n_samples, *, probabilities=None)
docs.text: Return the exact stationary variance of an n_samples time average.

docs.title: integrated_autocorrelation_time(chain, observable, *, probabilities=None)
docs.text: Return exact IAT as asymptotic variance divided by stationary variance.

docs.title: lag_covariance(chain, observable, lag, *, probabilities=None)
docs.text: Return Cov_pi(f(X_0), f(X_lag)) exactly.

docs.title: poisson_martingale_decomposition(chain, observable, states, *, probabilities=None)
docs.text:
Decompose a path sum using the exact Poisson solution.

If Lu = f - pi[f] and Delta M_k = u(X_{k+1}) - P u(X_k), then

sum_{k=0}^{n-1}(f(X_k)-pi[f]) = u(X_n)-u(X_0)-M_n.

The returned residual measures only floating-point error; the identity is algebraic and does not rely on stationarity of the supplied trajectory.

docs.title: solve_poisson_equation(chain, observable, *, probabilities=None)
docs.text:
Solve the centered finite-state Poisson equation exactly.

The package convention is L = P - I. The additive constant is fixed by pi[u] = 0. Algebraically, the solve uses the nonsingular fundamental matrix I - P + 1 pi and then flips sign.

docs.title: sampler_lab.mcmc
docs.text: Simulation-oriented Markov chain Monte Carlo algorithms.

docs.title: MCMCTrajectory dataclass
docs.text: Immutable array-valued trajectory and transition-level diagnostics.

docs.title: acceptance_rate property
docs.text: Observed acceptance fraction, or None for rejection-free kernels.

docs.title: samples(*, discard=0, thin=1)
docs.text: Return retained states after discarding and thinning state indices.

docs.title: observable_values(observable, *, discard=0, thin=1)
docs.text: Evaluate a scalar observable on retained states.

docs.title: BlockGibbsKernel dataclass
docs.text: Apply one exact conditional block update per transition.

docs.title: ConditionalUpdate
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

An exact draw from one conditional block given the complement.

docs.title: apply(state, rng, *, counter=None)
docs.text: Return a state with one conditional block resampled.

docs.title: DeterministicSweepGibbsKernel dataclass
docs.text: Apply a fixed sequence of conditional blocks in one macro-transition.

docs.title: FunctionalConditionalUpdate dataclass
docs.text: Adapt a function into a conditional-update object.

docs.title: RandomScanGibbsKernel dataclass
docs.text: Choose a conditional block independently at each transition.

docs.title: TransformedGibbsKernel dataclass
docs.text: Conjugate a Gibbs kernel through an invertible coordinate transform.

docs.title: MetropolisHastingsKernel dataclass
docs.text: One-step Metropolis--Hastings kernel for array-valued states.

docs.title: step(state, rng)
docs.text: Propose once and retain the current state after rejection.

docs.title: CoordinateGaussianRandomWalkProposal dataclass
docs.text:
Randomly perturb one coordinate with a symmetric Gaussian increment.

The transition density is interpreted with respect to the mixture of the one-dimensional coordinate reference measures. Proposals produced by this object differ from the source in exactly one coordinate almost surely.

docs.title: GaussianIndependenceProposal dataclass
docs.text: State-independent diagonal Gaussian proposal.

docs.title: GaussianRandomWalkProposal dataclass
docs.text: Diagonal Gaussian random walk Y = X + scale * Z.

docs.title: MultivariateGaussianRandomWalkProposal dataclass
docs.text: Full-covariance Gaussian random walk Y = X + L Z.

docs.title: Proposal
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

A proposal kernel with an explicit transition density or mass.

docs.title: sample(state, rng, *, counter=None)
docs.text: Draw a proposal from q(. | state).

docs.title: log_transition_density(to_state, from_state, *, counter=None)
docs.text: Return log q(to_state | from_state).

docs.title: StateDependentGaussianProposal dataclass
docs.text:
Diagonal Gaussian proposal with state-dependent mean and scale.

This class deliberately evaluates both the forward and reverse parameters, making proposal asymmetry visible in the Metropolis--Hastings ratio.

docs.title: run_chain(kernel, initial_state, rng, *, n_steps)
docs.text: Run a Markov kernel and store every state, including rejection repeats.

docs.title: log_metropolis_hastings_ratio(*, current_log_target, proposed_log_target, forward_log_proposal, reverse_log_proposal)
docs.text:
Return the logarithm of the untruncated MH acceptance ratio.

The current state must lie in the target support and a sampled proposal must have positive forward proposal density. A proposal outside target support or without a possible reverse transition is assigned ratio -inf.

docs.title: sampler_lab.annealing
docs.text: Annealed paths, Jarzynski weighting, and free-energy estimation.

docs.title: FreeEnergyEstimate dataclass
docs.text:
Normalization-ratio estimate expressed as a free-energy difference.

The convention is dimensionless free energy F = -log Z. Therefore delta_free_energy = -log(Z_final / Z_initial).

docs.title: AnnealedImportanceResult dataclass
docs.text:
Complete history of annealed importance sampling or annealed SMC.

weighted_clouds[k] is the population after reweighting from schedule value k to k + 1 and before any resampling or mutation. clouds[k + 1] is the corresponding post-resampling, post-mutation cloud.

docs.title: final_reduced_work property
docs.text: Full reduced work W = -sum(delta log gamma) per final trajectory.

docs.title: iid_jarzynski_estimate()
docs.text:
Return the IID Jarzynski/free-energy estimate for an unresampled run.

Resampling couples and duplicates trajectories, so the elementary IID standard-error formula is deliberately unavailable in that case.

docs.title: AnnealingPath
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Unnormalized log density indexed by a path parameter in [0, 1].

docs.title: log_unnormalized(state, beta)
docs.text: Evaluate log gamma_beta(state) up to a beta-dependent constant.

docs.title: BatchAnnealingPath
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Optional vectorized path-density capability.

docs.title: log_unnormalized_batch(states, beta)
docs.text: Evaluate one path density value per leading-axis state.

docs.title: BatchLogDensity
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

Optional vectorized log-density capability.

docs.title: log_prob_batch(states)
docs.text: Evaluate one log density per leading-axis state.

docs.title: FunctionalAnnealingPath dataclass
docs.text: Adapt a scalar function into an annealing path.

docs.title: GeometricAnnealingPath dataclass
docs.text: Geometric bridge gamma_beta = gamma_0^(1-beta) gamma_1^beta.

docs.title: AnnealingSchedule dataclass
docs.text:
A strictly increasing path parameter running from zero to one.

The array contains both endpoints. Consequently n_steps is one less than the number of stored values.

docs.title: n_steps property
docs.text: Number of annealing increments.

docs.title: increments property
docs.text: Successive path-parameter increments.

docs.title: linear(n_steps) classmethod
docs.text: Equally spaced path parameters.

docs.title: power(n_steps, exponent) classmethod
docs.text:
Return beta_k = (k / n_steps) ** exponent.

Exponents larger than one spend more stages near the initial law; exponents below one spend more stages near the final law.

docs.title: move(particles, beta, rng)

docs.title: free_energy_from_log_ratio(log_ratio)

docs.title: jarzynski_estimate(reduced_work)
docs.text:
Estimate a free-energy difference from independent reduced-work values.

Reduced work follows E[exp(-W)] = Z_final / Z_initial.

docs.title: annealed_importance_sampling(initial_particles, path, schedule, transition, rng, *, resampler=None, resample_every_step=False, resample_ess_fraction=None, target_particle_count=None)
docs.text:
Estimate Z_1 / Z_0 along a path of unnormalized distributions.

Each stage first applies the exact density-ratio increment at the current particle locations, optionally resamples, and then applies a transition intended to preserve the new path distribution. Without resampling this is ordinary annealed importance sampling/Jarzynski weighting. With resampling it is annealed sequential Monte Carlo.

docs.text: Evaluate a scalar path density for every leading-axis particle.

docs.title: incremental_log_weights(path, particles, beta_from, beta_to)
docs.text: Return log gamma_beta_to - log gamma_beta_from for each particle.

docs.title: validate_path_parameter(beta)
docs.text: Validate one path parameter.

docs.title: MassMatrix dataclass
docs.text:
Constant positive-definite momentum covariance M.

The kinetic energy is K(p) = p^T M^{-1} p / 2 and momenta are sampled from N(0, M). For a Gaussian target with precision Kappa, choosing M = Kappa equalizes Hamiltonian frequencies; this convention is worth stating because some software exposes the inverse mass instead.

docs.title: evaluate_at(state)
docs.text: Return A(x) and div A(x).

docs.title: HamiltonianMonteCarloKernel dataclass

Bases: _HamiltonianKernelBase

Position-valued HMC with fresh Gaussian momentum each transition.

docs.title: HamiltonianPhaseDensity dataclass
docs.text: Phase-space density proportional to exp(-H(q,p)).

docs.title: LeapfrogMomentumFlipInvolution dataclass
docs.text: The involution R Phi formed from leapfrog and momentum flip.

docs.title: PersistentHamiltonianKernel dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: _HamiltonianKernelBase

Generalized HMC on phase space with partial momentum refreshment.

The numerical flow is accepted without a terminal momentum flip. On rejection, refreshed momentum is flipped. This is the transformed-rejection form obtained by applying momentum flip after an involutive R Phi Metropolis proposal.

docs.title: UnadjustedHamiltonianKernel dataclass
docs.text:
Bases: _HamiltonianKernelBase

Fresh-momentum numerical Hamiltonian trajectories without MH correction.

Bases: Protocol

Deterministic proposal map equal to its own inverse.

docs.title: apply(state)
docs.text: Apply the involution.

docs.title: log_abs_det_jacobian(state)
docs.text: Return the logarithm of the absolute Jacobian determinant.

docs.title: InvolutiveMetropolisKernel dataclass
docs.text:
Metropolis correction for a deterministic involution.

Correctness requires the supplied map to be an involution. The Jacobian term permits non-volume-preserving involutions; volume-preserving reflections and momentum-flipped leapfrog maps simply return zero.

docs.title: MomentumFlipInvolution dataclass
docs.text: Canonical momentum flip (q,p) -> (q,-p).

docs.title: ConstantPreconditioner dataclass
docs.text: Constant symmetric positive-definite Langevin preconditioner.

docs.title: FunctionalPreconditioner dataclass
docs.text: Position-dependent preconditioner with an explicit divergence function.

docs.title: MetropolisAdjustedLangevinKernel dataclass
docs.text:
Metropolized Euler proposal for preconditioned Langevin dynamics.

With a constant identity preconditioner this is ordinary MALA. With a matrix field it remains a valid Metropolis--Hastings kernel because both state-dependent Gaussian proposal densities are evaluated explicitly.

docs.title: step(state, rng)
docs.text: Propose one Langevin step and apply the MH correction.

docs.title: Preconditioner

Bases: Protocol

docs.title: divergence_at(state)
docs.text: Return div M with component sum_j partial_j M_ij.

docs.title: cholesky_at(state)
docs.text: Return a lower Cholesky factor of M(x).

docs.title: log_determinant_at(state)
docs.text: Return log det M(x).

docs.title: PreconditionerEvaluation dataclass
docs.text: One internally consistent evaluation of a matrix field and its geometry.

docs.title: UnadjustedLangevinKernel dataclass
docs.text: Euler discretization of preconditioned overdamped Langevin dynamics.

docs.title: step(state, rng)
docs.text: Advance one ULA step with covariance 2 h M(x).

docs.title: MetropolizedUnderdampedLangevinKernel dataclass
docs.text:
OU momentum refresh followed by generalized HMC correction.

Accepted proposals retain the forward leapfrog momentum. Rejections transform the refreshed state by momentum flip. This transformed rejection is essential: simply retaining momentum after a failed persistent trajectory does not satisfy the generalized detailed-balance construction.

docs.title: UnderdampedGeneratorComponents dataclass
docs.text: Hamiltonian (skew), OU (symmetric), and total generator values.

docs.title: UnderdampedLangevinKernel dataclass
docs.text: BAOAB splitting for underdamped Langevin dynamics on phase space.

docs.text: KL divergence between equal-mean Gaussians in the stated direction.

docs.title: gaussian_quadratic_expectation(mean, covariance, *, quadratic=None, linear=None, constant=0.0)
docs.text: Evaluate E[X^T B X + c^T X + constant] exactly.

docs.title: gaussian_ula_analysis(target, step_size, *, preconditioner=None)
docs.text: Return exact constant-preconditioned ULA stability and invariant bias.

docs.title: linear_gaussian_iat(transition, stationary_covariance, observable_vector)
docs.text: Exact IAT of a centered linear observable for a stable Gaussian AR(1).

docs.title: solve_discrete_lyapunov(transition, noise_covariance)
docs.text: Solve C = A C A^T + Q by vectorization using NumPy only.

docs.title: diffusion_generator_value(drift, covariance, gradient, hessian, x)
docs.text: Evaluate b dot grad(f) + 1/2 tr(a Hess(f)) at one point.

docs.title: estimate_discrete_generator(kernel, test_function, x, step_size, n_replications, rng)
docs.text:
Estimate the scaled one-step generator of kernel at x.

Every replication starts from the same state, so this estimates a conditional expectation rather than a time average from one trajectory.

docs.title: estimate_local_moments(kernel, x, step_size, n_replications, rng)
docs.text:
Estimate local drift and infinitesimal covariance from repeated one-step moves.

second_moment_rate estimates E[Delta X Delta X^T | X=x] / h. centered_covariance_rate removes the empirical mean increment. Both converge to the diffusion covariance as h -> 0; the raw second moment is the quantity appearing directly in the Kramers--Moyal limit.

docs.title: estimate_poisson_invariant_bias(kernel, poisson_solution, continuous_generator_on_solution, stationary_samples, step_size, rng, *, replications_per_state=1)
docs.text:
Estimate an invariant-measure bias through the Poisson identity.

If L g = f - pi(f) and the supplied states follow an invariant law pi_h of kernel, then

pi_h(f) - pi(f) = pi_h[(L - (P_h-I)/h) g].

The routine estimates the right-hand side. It does not pretend the input states are independent when they come from a chain; callers remain responsible for a time-series uncertainty analysis in that case.

docs.title: canonical_symplectic_matrix(dimension)
docs.text: Return the canonical 2d x 2d symplectic matrix.

docs.title: conservative_skew_drift(state, energy_gradient, skew_field)
docs.text:
Return A grad(H) - div(A) for a skew-symmetric field A.

Under ordinary boundary conditions this drift preserves density proportional to exp(-H). The divergence correction vanishes for the canonical constant symplectic matrix used by ordinary Hamiltonian mechanics.

docs.title: finite_difference_jacobian(mapping, state, *, epsilon=1e-06)
docs.text: Central finite-difference Jacobian for deterministic-map diagnostics.

docs.title: gaussian_exact_flow_matrix(target, integration_time, *, mass=None)
docs.text: Exact Gaussian Hamiltonian flow matrix in centered phase coordinates.

docs.title: gaussian_hamiltonian_analysis(target, step_size, n_steps, *, mass=None)
docs.text: Analyze leapfrog stability and the corresponding exact-flow HMC map.

docs.title: gaussian_hamiltonian_frequencies(target, mass=None)
docs.text: Return normal-mode frequencies for a Gaussian Hamiltonian system.

docs.title: gaussian_leapfrog_matrix(target, step_size, n_steps=1, *, mass=None)
docs.text: Exact linear phase map of leapfrog on a centered Gaussian target.

docs.title: symplecticity_error(jacobian)
docs.text: Frobenius residual of J.T Omega J = Omega.

docs.title: volume_preservation_error(jacobian)
docs.text: Return abs(log |det J|); zero characterizes local volume preservation.

docs.title: partially_refresh_momentum(momentum, mass, rng, *, persistence, counter=None)
docs.text:
Apply an exact Gaussian momentum-refreshment kernel.

persistence=0 is a full refresh and persistence=1 leaves momentum unchanged. Every intermediate value preserves N(0, M).

docs.title: randomized_trajectory_steps(rng, *, minimum, maximum)
docs.text: Draw an inclusive integer trajectory length for resonance avoidance.

docs.title: leapfrog_integrate(system, state, step_size, n_steps, *, position_map=None)
docs.text:
Integrate a separable Hamiltonian with velocity Verlet / leapfrog.

The implementation uses n_steps + 1 target-gradient evaluations by reusing the interior half steps. Negative step sizes are allowed so that time-reversibility can be tested directly.

docs.title: leapfrog_map(system, state, step_size, n_steps, *, position_map=None)
docs.text: Array-valued wrapper around :func:leapfrog_integrate.

docs.title: leapfrog_reversibility_error(system, state, step_size, n_steps, *, position_map=None, difference_map=None)
docs.text:
Forward/backward integration error in phase-space Euclidean norm.

difference_map can supply a periodic difference for toroidal positions.

docs.title: momentum_flip(state)
docs.text: Flip momentum while retaining position.

docs.title: involution_error(involution, state)
docs.text: Euclidean residual of applying an alleged involution twice.

docs.title: log_involutive_metropolis_ratio(*, current_log_density, proposed_log_density, log_abs_det_jacobian)

docs.text: Evaluate a full-rank multivariate Gaussian transition density.

docs.title: overdamped_langevin_drift(target, state, *, preconditioner=None, include_divergence=True)
docs.text: Return M grad(log pi) + div M for the preconditioned diffusion.

docs.title: ornstein_uhlenbeck_momentum_step(momentum, mass, rng, *, friction, duration, counter=None)
docs.text: Advance the exact OU momentum subflow preserving N(0, M).

docs.title: ornstein_uhlenbeck_persistence(friction, duration)
docs.text: Return exp(-friction * duration) for the exact momentum OU flow.

docs.title: underdamped_generator_value(target, state, gradient_position, gradient_momentum, hessian_momentum, *, mass, friction)
docs.text:
Evaluate the underdamped generator on supplied derivatives of a test function.

For dq = M^{-1}p dt and dp = grad(log pi(q)) dt - gamma p dt + sqrt(2 gamma M) dW, the Hamiltonian contribution is skew-adjoint and the OU contribution is symmetric in the canonical phase-space law.

docs.title: StochasticNewtonProposal dataclass
docs.text:
Local Gaussian approximation with explicit overdamped-Langevin scaling.

For local metric S(x) = [-D^2 log pi(x)]^{-1}, the proposal is

Y = x + h S(x) grad log pi(x) + sqrt(2 h S(x)) Z.

The divergence correction is intentionally omitted. Metropolization restores exact invariance and keeps the proposal practical when derivatives of S are unavailable.

docs.title: StretchMoveKernel dataclass
docs.text: Goodman--Weare stretch move on the full ensemble Markov state.

docs.title: WalkMoveKernel dataclass
docs.text:
Symmetric ensemble walk move using centered complementary walkers.

A proposal for walker i is X_i + scale/sqrt(s) * sum z_j (X_j - mean) where the s complementary walkers are selected without replacement and z_j are standard normals. Conditional on the complementary ensemble the proposal is symmetric, so the Hastings ratio is just the target-density ratio.

docs.title: ensemble_effective_sample_size(values)
docs.text:
Compute ESS from the ensemble-average time series.

If equilibrium walkers are independent, Var(mean_j f(X_j)) = Var(f)/L; therefore the target-draw-equivalent ESS is L * N / IAT.

docs.title: mean_cross_walker_correlation(values)

docs.text:
Power-law stochastic-approximation schedule.

rate(t) = initial_rate / (offset + t + 1) ** exponent. Exponents in (1/2, 1] satisfy the usual square-summable but not summable conditions used by Robbins--Monro algorithms.

docs.title: FrozenAdaptiveRandomWalkKernel dataclass
docs.text: Fixed Gaussian random-walk MH kernel produced after warmup.

docs.title: LinearBaseline dataclass
docs.text: Ridge-regularized least-squares baseline with an intercept.

docs.title: RunningMeanBaseline dataclass
docs.text: Online scalar baseline equal to the mean return seen so far.

docs.title: ZeroBaseline dataclass
docs.text: No variance reduction.

docs.title: GeneralizedSpeedGradient dataclass
docs.text: One reparameterized objective and gradient evaluation.

docs.title: GeneralizedSpeedTrainingResult dataclass

docs.title: NaturalGradientResult dataclass
docs.text: Damped natural-gradient direction and local KL prediction.

docs.title: ReinforceEstimate dataclass
docs.text: REINFORCE gradient and variance diagnostics.

docs.title: PolicyMixtureProposal dataclass
docs.text:
State-dependent proposal mixture with its marginal density exposed.

Sampling first draws a latent component action, but the MH correction uses the marginal mixture density in both directions. This is the exact way to use state-dependent component weights; simply selecting among already-corrected kernels with state-dependent probabilities is generally invalid.

docs.title: AcceptanceObjective dataclass
docs.text: Acceptance-only baseline, intentionally vulnerable to zero-size proposals.

docs.title: AcceptedSquaredJumpObjective dataclass
docs.text: Accepted squared jump in Euclidean or user-supplied Mahalanobis geometry.

docs.title: ContrastiveDivergenceLowerBoundObjective dataclass
docs.text:
One-proposal lower-bound reward for contrastive divergence.

With Metropolis acceptance probability a, target log-ratio delta, and forward proposal log density log q(y|x), the reward is

a * delta - a * log(a) - a * log q(y|x).

The final two terms lower-bound the entropy of the MH transition kernel; the omitted rejection-atom entropy is nonnegative.

docs.title: CostNormalizedObjective dataclass
docs.text: Divide another reward by a declared operation-cost model.

docs.title: FeatureJumpObjective dataclass
docs.text: Accepted squared displacement in declared diagnostic features.

docs.title: GeneralizedSpeedObjective dataclass
docs.text:
Sampled lower bound on log generalized speed.

For proposal entropy H and Metropolis ratio r, this returns min(0, log r) + beta * H. Unlike accepted-jump rewards, it learns from rejected proposals as well.

docs.title: OperationCostWeights dataclass
docs.text: Linear cost model for reward normalization.

docs.title: PolicyTransition dataclass
docs.text: One proposal, acceptance decision, and policy-side diagnostics.

docs.title: SGD dataclass
docs.text: Plain stochastic-gradient ascent.

docs.title: Adam dataclass
docs.text: Adam stochastic-gradient ascent on a flat parameter vector.

docs.title: FrozenLinearSoftmaxPolicy dataclass
docs.text: Immutable categorical policy used by frozen evaluation kernels.

docs.title: LinearSoftmaxPolicy
docs.text: Categorical policy with logits weights @ features.

docs.title: score(features, action_index)
docs.text: Return grad log pi(action | features) in row-major parameter order.

docs.title: PolicyAction dataclass
docs.text: Sampled policy action with its score-function derivative.

docs.title: SquashedGaussianPolicy
docs.text: Diagonal Gaussian policy transformed into a finite action box.

Evaluate the sampled lower bound and its pathwise gradient.

For y = x + exp(log_scale) * noise, the objective is

docs.title: laplace_log_integral(points, epsilon)
docs.text:
Approximate integral a(x) exp(-I(x)/epsilon) dx.

Each supplied point must be an isolated, nondegenerate interior minimizer. Multiple minimizers are summed in the log domain rather than replaced by the smallest prefactor.

docs.title: estimate_with_mixture(problem, proposal, rng, size, *, counter=None)
docs.text: Estimate a Gaussian rare-event probability under a finite shift mixture.

docs.title: exact_symmetric_mixture_log_second_moment(problem, epsilon, *, quadrature_order=192, tail_limit=40.0)
docs.text:
Compute the exact second moment for the equal two-dominating-point mixture.

Orthogonal Gaussian coordinates integrate out. A change of variables reduces the remaining half-line integral to a smooth exponentially decaying integral on [0, inf). Gauss--Legendre quadrature on [0, tail_limit] is deterministic and stable even when the probability itself is far below floating-point range.

docs.title: symmetric_twist_mixture(problem, epsilon)
docs.text: Place equal Gaussian components at both dominating points.

docs.title: standard_normal_log_upper_tail(x)
docs.text:
Return log P(Z >= x) for a standard normal Z.

erfc is accurate in the central region. In the far positive tail an asymptotic Mills-ratio expansion avoids underflow. Negative arguments are handled by complementing the corresponding positive-tail probability.

docs.title: standard_normal_upper_tail(x)
docs.text: Return P(Z >= x) while preserving the log-tail implementation.

docs.title: select_shift_scale(problem, epsilon, candidates)

docs.text: Choose a Gaussian temperature by exact second-moment minimization.

docs.title: estimate_from_log_contributions(log_contributions, *, event_count=None, counter=None)
docs.text:
Summarize nonnegative IID contributions represented by logarithms.

Zero contributions are represented by -inf. The contribution ESS is (sum y_i)^2 / sum y_i^2 and is deliberately distinct from ordinary importance-weight ESS: it measures how many observations actually support this estimator.

docs.title: FunnelTarget dataclass
docs.text:
Neal-style funnel with coordinate anisotropy and orthogonal rotation.

In latent coordinates y=(v,z),

v ~ N(0, sigma_v^2) and z_i | v ~ N(0, s_i^2 exp(v)). Observed coordinates satisfy x = location + rotation @ y.

docs.title: IsingExactDistribution dataclass
docs.text: Enumerated finite-lattice Ising law.

docs.title: IsingGibbsPopulationTransition dataclass
docs.text:
Vectorized deterministic Gibbs sweeps for an annealed Ising population.

path_beta is mapped linearly from initial_beta to final_beta. Every site update is vectorized across particles while sites themselves are visited in lexicographic order, matching deterministic-sweep Gibbs.

docs.title: IsingModel dataclass
docs.text:
Periodic square-lattice Ising model.

The unnormalized log density is

beta * (coupling * sum_<i,j> s_i s_j + field * sum_i s_i).

Each undirected nearest-neighbor bond is counted once via right and down neighbors. On a two-site periodic axis this retains the natural torus multiplicity, so every site still has four incident bond directions.

docs.title: log_prob(state)
docs.text: Return the unnormalized target log density.

docs.title: log_prob_batch(states)
docs.text: Evaluate the unnormalized log density for a particle population.

docs.title: conditional_plus_probability(state, site)
docs.text: Return P(s_site = +1 | all other spins) stably.

docs.title: log_flip_ratio(state, site)
docs.text: Return log pi(flipped) - log pi(current) using only neighbors.

docs.title: IsingSiteGibbsUpdate dataclass
docs.text: Exact conditional resampling of one Ising spin.

docs.title: RandomScanIsingMetropolisKernel dataclass
docs.text: Uniform single-spin flip Metropolis kernel using a local ratio.

docs.title: RosenbrockTarget dataclass
docs.text:
Two-dimensional Rosenbrock probability density.

The unnormalized log density is

.. math::

\log \pi(x, y) = -\frac{a(y-x^2)^2 + (b-x)^2}{s}.

The defaults a=100, b=1, and s=20 produce a narrow curved target with an exact hierarchical representation

X ~ N(b, s/2) and Y | X ~ N(X**2, s/(2a)).

docs.title: sample_exact(rng, size)
docs.text: Draw independent exact samples from the hierarchical representation.

docs.text: Return the exact target mean.

docs.title: exact_covariance()
docs.text: Return the exact covariance obtained from Gaussian moments.

docs.title: SelfAvoidingWalkProposal dataclass
docs.text: Uniformly extend each path among its currently unvisited neighbors.

docs.title: XYModel dataclass
docs.text:
Nearest-neighbor periodic square-lattice XY target.

For angles theta on an L x L torus,

log pi(theta) = beta * [J sum_<ij> cos(theta_i-theta_j) + h sum_i cos(theta_i)].

The density is unnormalized. Positions supplied to HMC should be wrapped with :func:wrap_angles after each drift substep so the state space remains the compact torus rather than an improper periodic lift to Euclidean space.

docs.title: interaction_sum(angles)
docs.text: Return the sum over rightward and downward periodic bonds.

docs.title: exact_single_site_mean_cosine()
docs.text:
Exact one-site field response, valid when size=1.

Self-bond terms are constant at one site, so the nontrivial law is von Mises with concentration beta * external_field.

docs.title: available_self_avoiding_neighbors(path, *, periodic_size=None)
docs.text: Return unvisited nearest neighbors of the final point in path.

docs.title: count_self_avoiding_walks(n_steps, *, periodic_size=None)
docs.text:
Exactly count fixed-origin square-lattice self-avoiding walks.

This depth-first enumerator is intended for small validation cases, not record-setting enumeration. n_steps counts edges, so the path contains n_steps + 1 vertices.

docs.title: is_self_avoiding_walk(path, *, periodic_size=None)
docs.text: Check uniqueness and nearest-neighbor adjacency of a lattice path.

docs.title: sample_self_avoiding_walks(rng, *, n_steps, n_particles, resampling=None, resample_every_step=False, resample_ess_fraction=None, periodic_size=None)
docs.text:
Estimate and sample the uniform fixed-origin self-avoiding-walk law.

The proposal is the Rosenbluth growth rule. Its incremental importance weight is the number of currently available neighbors. Consequently the returned normalizing-constant estimate targets the exact walk count.

docs.title: modified_bessel_i0_i1(value, *, tolerance=1e-15)
docs.text:
Compute I_0(x) and I_1(x) by convergent power series.

This is intended for validation-scale concentrations rather than extreme asymptotics. The recurrence avoids factorial construction and is accurate for the moderate fields used in the XY laboratory.

docs.title: periodic_angle_difference(first, second)
docs.text: Return the shortest signed componentwise angular difference.

docs.title: von_mises_mean_cosine(concentration)
docs.text: Return E[cos(theta)] = I_1(kappa) / I_0(kappa).

docs.title: wrap_angles(angles)
docs.text: Wrap angles componentwise to [-pi, pi).

docs.title: Capability-Aware Continuous Benchmarking
docs.text: The common benchmark compares continuous samplers without pretending that all outputs have the same semantics. Independent draws, weighted particles, stationary chains, ensembles, and variational particle clouds can share target-level accuracy metrics, but they do not share every mixing diagnostic or exactness guarantee.

docs.title: Command line
docs.text:
List available targets and methods:

sampler-lab-benchmark --list

Run the complete replicated suite:

sampler-lab-benchmark \
  --samples 2000 \
  --warmup 500 \
  --reference-samples 1000 \
  --replicates 3 \
  --output-dir benchmark_report \
  --figures

Use --targets and --methods with comma-separated names to select a subset. --quick reduces all internal budgets for a smoke run. --json prints the complete structured result, and --fail-fast converts a recorded runtime failure into an immediate exception.

docs.title: Reference targets
docs.text:
The default suite forms a diagnostic ladder:

  1. Correlated Gaussian: exact linear-algebra oracle for covariance and conditioning.
  2. Separated anisotropic Gaussian mixture: exposes mode trapping and reverse-KL collapse.
  3. Rotated anisotropic funnel: exposes position-dependent geometry and neck/tail failures.
  4. Bimodal anisotropic funnel: combines global mode discovery with severe local curvature.

Every target has a normalized density, exact direct sampler, analytic gradient and Hessian, and known first two moments. Mixture targets also expose exact mode labels and probabilities.

docs.title: Output semantics and exactness
docs.text:
Every adapter returns an explicit output_semantics label. Current labels include:

  • iid-samples;
  • markov-chain;
  • ensemble-chain;
  • weighted-samples;
  • weighted-particles;
  • variational-samples;
  • deterministic-particles.

exact_after_freeze means that, after all warmup or learning has stopped, the evaluated method preserves or exactly corrects to the requested target. It does not mean independent samples, rapid convergence, or good finite-budget mode coverage. Reverse-KL and SVGD outputs are marked approximate. A frozen variational proposal followed by full independence Metropolis correction is marked exact.

docs.title: Capability checks
docs.text:
Sampler adapters declare density, gradient, Hessian, conditional, direct-sample, multimodality, and state-space requirements. Targets declare the access they provide. Incompatible pairings are written to the report with all exclusion reasons rather than omitted or assigned fabricated scores.

Model-specific discrete methods, unit-disk samplers, and self-avoiding-walk estimators therefore remain in their own exact laboratories. “Not applicable” is a result; forcing every method into one leaderboard would be a category error with nicer typography.

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
All representations are compared against one exact reference sample per target and replicate. Common distributional diagnostics include:

  • standardized mean error;
  • relative covariance error;
  • inverse-multiquadric reference-sample MMD;
  • mode-occupancy error when exact labels exist;
  • funnel-neck, funnel-mouth, and latent-scale errors.

Stationary chains and ensemble trajectories additionally receive acceptance and mode-residence, first-passage, switching, and round-trip diagnostics where meaningful. Weighted outputs receive weight ESS and maximum normalized weight. Deterministic particle methods do not receive fictional chain IATs.

The report deliberately has no overall winner score. The Pareto summary uses IMQ-MMD and total training-plus-evaluation wall time as a compact view; the detailed rows retain target access, output semantics, exactness, training cost, evaluation cost, and target-specific failures.

docs.title: Cost accounting
docs.text:
Training and frozen evaluation use separate OperationCounter objects and wall clocks. Reported operations include log-density, proposal-density, gradient, Hessian, matrix-factorization, policy, and random-draw costs when the implementation can count them directly. Counts are algorithmic work proxies, not claims of equal hardware cost.

Warmup, variational fitting, and policy learning cannot quietly disappear into “free” samples. Amortized comparisons can be derived from the JSON report for any declared number of downstream evaluation draws.

docs.title: Reproducibility
docs.text:
A base seed is deterministically split by target, method, replicate, and purpose. Methods in one replicate share the same exact reference sample but receive disjoint stochastic streams. Training and frozen evaluation streams are also disjoint inside learned adapters.

The canonical report bundle contains:

  • benchmark_results.json: complete nested results, aggregates, exclusions, and failures;
  • benchmark_results.csv: flattened per-replicate rows;
  • benchmark_summary.md: compact aggregate table and Pareto frontiers;
  • manifest.json: package version, seed, replicate count, and filenames;
  • optional reference figures.

The full benchmark is an opt-in experiment. CI checks deterministic identities and small fixed-seed adapter runs; it does not require a stochastic total ordering between methods.

docs.title: Python API
docs.text:
from sampler_lab.benchmarks import BenchmarkConfig, run_benchmark_suite, write_report_bundle

suite = run_benchmark_suite(
    config=BenchmarkConfig(n_samples=1000, warmup_steps=250, reference_samples=1000),
    n_replicates=3,
    seed=2022,
    target_names=("bimodal-anisotropic-funnel",),
)
write_report_bundle(suite, "benchmark_report")

Custom methods should implement the ContinuousSamplerAdapter protocol and return a validated SamplerOutput. Custom targets should use ContinuousTargetCase and declare their capabilities.

docs.title: Phase 0 - Infrastructure
docs.text:
• src/ package layout and project metadata;
  • explicit RNG construction and child-stream spawning;
  • operation counters;
  • stable logsumexp and log-weight normalization;
  • target, independent-sampler, Markov-kernel, and resampler protocols;
  • GitHub Actions configuration for Ruff, mypy, and pytest.

docs.title: Phase 1 - Foundations and exact sampling
docs.text:
• IID estimates and empirical error decomposition;
  • inverse-CDF and finite generalized-inverse sampling;
  • polar transforms and Box-Muller normals;
  • generic log-domain rejection sampling with envelope validation;
  • normalized Gaussian target with gradient and Hessian;
  • direct and rejection unit-disk samplers;
  • exact-sampling documentation and disk benchmark CLI.

docs.title: Phase 2 - Importance sampling
docs.text:
• explicit log-weight construction with proposal-support checks;
  • standard and self-normalized importance estimates;
  • delta-method standard errors and SNIS bias approximation;
  • stable normalizing-constant ratio estimates;
  • weighted moments and weight-quality diagnostics;
  • scale-invariant chi-squared and order-two Renyi divergence estimates;
  • Gaussian upper-tail proposal comparison;
  • high-dimensional Gaussian weight-collapse experiment;
  • sampler-lab-importance-demo CLI.

docs.title: Phase 3 - Particle methods
docs.text:
• immutable particle clouds with normalized log weights;
  • separate pre-resampling and post-resampling histories;
  • generic sequential proposal interface and SIS recursion;
  • stable products of incremental normalizing-constant estimates;
  • multinomial resampling;
  • systematic resampling;
  • floor-plus-Bernoulli resampling with variable population size;
  • exact marginal offspring-variance formulas;
  • offspring-count and unique-parent diagnostics;
  • ESS-triggered resampling;
  • variable-population ancestry maps, lineage tracing, and unique-ancestor histories;
  • explicit particle-extinction errors;
  • vectorized Rosenbluth self-avoiding-walk growth;
  • exact depth-first enumeration for small walk lengths;
  • sampler-lab-particle-demo comparison CLI.

docs.title: Phase 4 - Finite-state Markov theory
docs.text:
• row-stochastic transition operators acting on functions and measures;
  • exact operator duality checks;
  • communicating and closed-class decomposition;
  • extreme invariant distributions and uniqueness checks;
  • irreducibility, class periods, aperiodicity, and finite-state ergodicity;
  • global-balance and detailed-balance residuals;
  • stationary time reversal;
  • discrete generators L = P - I;
  • centered Poisson equations with residual diagnostics;
  • exact finite-path martingale decompositions;
  • exact lag autocovariances and autocorrelations;
  • exact finite-sample variance of stationary time averages;
  • exact asymptotic variance and integrated autocorrelation time;
  • Poincare, absolute spectral, and singular-value gap diagnostics;
  • finite-state Gibbs/partial-resampling kernels;
  • random-scan mixtures, deterministic compositions, and coordinate relabeling;
  • reversible, directed, and periodic ring-chain examples;
  • sampler-lab-markov-demo CLI with replicated finite-sample validation.

docs.title: Phase 5 - Gibbs and Metropolis--Hastings
docs.text:
• generic Metropolis--Hastings with explicit forward and reverse proposal terms;
  • retained repeated states after rejection;
  • immutable chain trajectories with transition-level diagnostics;
  • diagonal Gaussian random-walk and independence proposals;
  • coordinate Gaussian and state-dependent Gaussian proposals;
  • random-scan, deterministic-sweep, arbitrary-block, and transformed Gibbs kernels;
  • FFT empirical autocovariances and initial-positive-sequence IAT estimates;
  • periodic square-lattice Ising targets with exact local conditionals;
  • O(1) local single-spin Metropolis ratios;
  • exact small-lattice enumeration and partition functions;
  • exact random-scan Gibbs, deterministic-sweep Gibbs, and Metropolis matrices;
  • cost-normalized Ising benchmark with acceptance, magnetization, energy, IAT, ESS per spin update, and ESS per second;
  • sampler-lab-ising-demo CLI.

docs.title: Phase 6 - Annealed weighting and free energy
docs.text:
• validated linear, power, and custom annealing schedules;
  • geometric and functional paths of unnormalized distributions;
  • optional batched path-density evaluation;
  • stable incremental path log weights and cumulative reduced work;
  • Jarzynski/AIS normalization-ratio estimates;
  • dimensionless free-energy estimates with IID delta-method diagnostics;
  • annealed SMC with every-stage or ESS-triggered resampling;
  • separate pre-resampling and post-mutation particle histories;
  • path-space ancestry and full genealogical work histories;
  • intermediate-state reweighting toward later path laws;
  • vectorized deterministic Gibbs population sweeps for Ising paths;
  • exact small-lattice partition-ratio and magnetization validation;
  • sampler-lab-annealing-demo path-length and resampling CLI.

docs.title: Phase 7 - Generator-based dynamics and Langevin methods
docs.text:
• repeated-start empirical estimates of scaled discrete generators;
  • local drift, raw second-moment, and centered covariance-rate estimates;
  • analytic Itô diffusion-generator evaluation;
  • generic Euler--Maruyama kernels with rectangular diffusion factors;
  • constant and position-dependent positive-definite preconditioners;
  • explicit row-divergence corrections for state-dependent metrics;
  • unadjusted Langevin and full-covariance MALA kernels;
  • exact Gaussian ULA transition, stability boundary, and Lyapunov covariance;
  • exact Gaussian invariant-law KL bias and linear-observable IAT;
  • Poisson-equation invariant-bias estimation;
  • small-step random-walk Metropolis generator experiment;
  • conditioned-Gaussian ULA/MALA comparison CLI.

docs.title: Phase 8 - Hamiltonian and underdamped methods
docs.text:
• immutable position--momentum states and explicit Gaussian mass matrices;
  • canonical Hamiltonian systems, vector fields, energies, and operation accounting;
  • constant and position-dependent skew fields with row-divergence correction;
  • exact Gaussian Hamiltonian frequencies and flow matrices;
  • exact Gaussian leapfrog matrices and stability thresholds;
  • velocity-Verlet / leapfrog trajectories with reused interior gradients;
  • numerical reversibility, finite-difference volume, and symplectic diagnostics;
  • unadjusted Hamiltonian trajectories and fresh-momentum HMC;
  • partial Gaussian momentum refreshment and persistent generalized HMC;
  • deterministic involution protocol with explicit log-Jacobian corrections;
  • momentum-flip involutions and transformed rejection states;
  • exact Ornstein--Uhlenbeck momentum substeps;
  • skew/symmetric underdamped generator decomposition;
  • BAOAB underdamped Langevin splitting;
  • Metropolized underdamped dynamics with momentum-flipped rejections;
  • periodic square-lattice XY target, exact gradient, toroidal wrapping, and magnetization;
  • one-site von Mises exact-response validation;
  • conditioned-Gaussian and XY sampler-lab-hamiltonian-demo CLI.

docs.title: Phase 9 - Conditioning and affine-invariant ensembles
docs.text:
• invertible affine maps with point, batch, inverse, composition, and covariance transforms;
  • pushforward targets with exact log-density, gradient, and Hessian formulas;
  • Gaussian whitening, spectral condition numbers, and exact block-conditionals;
  • centered finite-difference Hessians from target gradients;
  • positive-definite Hessian repair by rejection, clipping, or absolute eigenvalues;
  • repair spectra and correction-norm diagnostics;
  • stochastic-Newton local Gaussian proposals with explicit overdamped-Langevin scaling;
  • specialized Metropolized stochastic Newton with reused endpoint geometry;
  • full-covariance Gaussian random-walk proposals;
  • immutable complete-ensemble states, transitions, and trajectories;
  • explicit affine-span rank and degenerate-population rejection;
  • sequential and split Goodman--Weare stretch moves;
  • sequential and split symmetric walk moves from complementary walkers;
  • per-walker acceptance, partner, cross-walker dependence, and ensemble ESS diagnostics;
  • exact coupled affine-equivariance tests for both ensemble moves;
  • parameterized Rosenbrock target with analytic derivatives, exact hierarchy, and exact moments;
  • conditioned-Gaussian and Rosenbrock sampler-lab-geometry-demo CLI.

docs.title: Phase 10 - Adaptive and policy-gradient sampling
docs.text:
• Welford running moments and regularized covariance estimates;
  • Robbins--Monro schedules, diminishing-adaptation diagnostics, and dual averaging;
  • explicit expanding warmup windows and detached serializable frozen policies;
  • adaptive full-covariance random-walk warmup with fresh post-freeze evaluation chains;
  • categorical softmax and bounded Gaussian proposal policies with analytic scores;
  • zero, running-mean, and linear policy-gradient baselines;
  • REINFORCE returns, exact categorical Fisher matrices, damped natural gradients, and local policy-KL trust regions;
  • acceptance-only failure baselines, accepted and feature-space jump rewards;
  • generalized-speed and contrastive-divergence lower-bound objectives;
  • operation-cost-normalized rewards with separate training and evaluation counters;
  • exact frozen state-independent kernel mixtures and density-correct state-dependent proposal mixtures;
  • reverse- and forward-KL diagonal Gaussian proposal fitting;
  • exact independence-MH correction of frozen variational proposals;
  • inverse-multiquadric KSD and Stein variational gradient descent;
  • normalized, exactly sampleable anisotropic Gaussian-mixture, rotated funnel, and bimodal- funnel targets with analytic derivatives;
  • capability-aware benchmark registration, exact reference sampling, mode diagnostics, MMD, and machine-readable benchmark results;
  • sampler-lab-policy-demo objective-gaming and separated-mixture CLI.

docs.title: Phase 11 - Rare events and small-noise importance sampling
docs.text:
• exact anisotropic Gaussian halfspace and symmetric two-sided rare-event problems;
  • exact probabilities, rate functions, and one or two dominating points;
  • stable standard-normal log survival probabilities in extreme tails;
  • ordinary multidimensional Laplace approximations with Hessian prefactors;
  • Gaussian boundary-Laplace approximations with explicit small-noise prefactors;
  • log-domain nonnegative-contribution estimates with absolute and relative uncertainty;
  • contribution ESS, maximum-contribution, event-count, and operation diagnostics;
  • exponential relative-variance rate fitting against 1 / epsilon;
  • shifted Gaussian linear exponential twisting with exact likelihood ratios;
  • exact shifted-proposal second moments for one- and two-sided events;
  • centered Gaussian temperature broadening with exact second moments;
  • fixed-covariance asymptotic temperature schedules and exact grid selection;
  • finite mixtures of Gaussian twists with stable logsumexp densities;
  • deterministic one-dimensional quadrature for the symmetric-mixture second moment;
  • deliberate single-twist failure on a two-dominating-point event;
  • sampler-lab-rare-event-demo text and JSON CLI.

docs.title: Release 0.12.0 - Cross-method benchmark and release polish
docs.text:
• common validated adapter output across IID, weighted, particle, Markov, ensemble, variational, deterministic-particle, and learned methods;
  • complete capability-aware adapter matrix for all compatible continuous samplers;
  • exact-reference correlated Gaussian, anisotropic mixture, rotated funnel, and bimodal-funnel target hierarchy;
  • weighted and unweighted mean, covariance, IMQ-MMD, mode, and funnel diagnostics;
  • separate training and frozen-evaluation operation counters and wall clocks;
  • deterministic seed derivation with shared per-replicate reference samples;
  • replicated aggregates with standard errors and accuracy-time Pareto summaries;
  • explicit incompatibility exclusions and preserved runtime failures;
  • JSON, flattened CSV, Markdown, manifest, and optional figure report artifacts;
  • sampler-lab-benchmark selection, quick-run, listing, JSON, and figure CLI;
  • public API, benchmarking semantics, changelog, and capability, provenance, and reference docs.

docs.title: Validation at v0.12.0
docs.text:
• Ruff lint: clean;
  • Ruff formatting: clean across 196 files;
  • mypy strict mode: clean across 122 package source files;
  • pytest: 279 passing tests (242 unit and 37 statistical);
  • package compilation: clean;
  • editable installation: successful;
  • installed package reports version 0.12.0;
  • all twelve installed console entry points resolved successfully;
  • the reference benchmark completed 108 method/target/replicate runs with two explicit incompatibility exclusions and zero runtime failures;
  • JSON, CSV, Markdown, pairing matrix, manifest, and twelve PNG figures were generated.

Statistical files were run in isolated processes, and two historically troublesome aggregate files were split by test node, because the environment still shows the documented pytest/plugin shutdown stall. All 37 statistical assertions completed successfully.

docs.title: Maintenance target
docs.text: The planned method families are implemented. Future work should be treated as maintenance or explicitly scoped extension, with the same exactness, documentation, operation-accounting, and failure-mode standards.

docs.title: Performance Expectations
docs.text: Sampler Lab optimizes first for inspectability, reproducibility, and mathematically explicit state. Many Markov kernels therefore advance one state at a time in Python. That is deliberate: proposal terms, rejected states, operation counts, adaptation boundaries, and diagnostics remain visible. The tradeoff is lower throughput than vectorized, compiled, JAX, or GPU-oriented libraries.

docs.title: Illustrative throughput
docs.text:
The committed quick benchmark uses 300 evaluation outputs per method and two fixed-seed replicates. On the environment that generated the reference artifact, the correlated-Gaussian case reported:

Method Mean evaluation time Approximate outputs per second Importance sampling 0.001897 s 158,000 Random-walk Metropolis 0.01806 s 16,600 MALA 0.03010 s 10,000 HMC 0.07698 s 3,900 Stochastic Newton 0.08287 s 3,600

These figures are orientation, not promises. The generating hardware was not recorded, targets have different evaluation costs, and one output from one method is not equivalent to one output from another. Prefer operation counts, ESS, error, and wall time together. Re-run sampler-lab-benchmark on the hardware and target that matter to you.

docs.title: Intended use
docs.text:
Sampler Lab is well suited to:

  • learning and auditing algorithm mechanics;
  • validating identities and invariance properties;
  • constructing reproducible small-to-medium experiments;
  • comparing method semantics and failure modes;
  • serving as a clear reference implementation before optimization.

It is not intended to beat specialized production samplers on raw throughput. Vectorized (n_chains, dimension) kernels and compiled backends are reasonable future extensions, but they must preserve explicit RNG ownership, rejection semantics, counters, and diagnostics rather than silently changing the contract.

docs.title: Public API and Stability Policy
docs.text:
Sampler Lab is a teaching and research codebase whose public surface is the package-level exports, validated dataclasses, protocols, and command-line entry points documented here. Internal helper functions beginning with _ are not stable.

Sampler Lab is a feature-complete beta. Backward-incompatible public changes before 1.0 must be documented in CHANGELOG.md, accompanied by migration notes, and tested at the package-export level. The installed package ships a py.typed marker, so downstream type checkers can consume the annotations under PEP 561.

docs.title: Core conventions
docs.text:
• Pass an explicit numpy.random.Generator to every stochastic operation.
  • Represent target densities and importance weights in log space.
  • Treat states, results, policies, and particle histories as immutable value objects where practical.
  • Return diagnostics and raw samples rather than hiding rejection, resampling, adaptation, or correction steps.
  • Keep training/warmup separate from frozen evaluation.
  • Use OperationCounter for comparable algorithmic work.

docs.title: Stable package families
docs.text:
Package Stable responsibility sampler_lab.core RNG splitting, protocols, numerics, linear algebra, counters, common results sampler_lab.estimators IID and weighted estimators, normalization, empirical error metrics sampler_lab.exact inversion, Box--Muller, transformations, rejection sampling sampler_lab.importance log weights, IS/SNIS, ratio estimates, divergences, collapse diagnostics sampler_lab.particles particle clouds, resampling, SIS/SMC histories, ancestry sampler_lab.markov finite-state operators, invariant laws, Poisson equations, exact asymptotics sampler_lab.mcmc generic MH, proposals, chain runners, Gibbs schedules sampler_lab.annealing annealed paths, AIS/Jarzynski, annealed SMC, free energies sampler_lab.dynamics diffusion generators, ULA/MALA, Hamiltonian and underdamped methods sampler_lab.geometry affine maps, preconditioners, Hessian repair, stochastic Newton sampler_lab.ensemble complete-ensemble states, stretch and walk moves, ensemble diagnostics sampler_lab.adaptive running moments, schedules, dual averaging, warmup windows sampler_lab.learning proposal policies, REINFORCE, objectives, variational fitting, Stein methods sampler_lab.rare_events small-noise problems, Laplace asymptotics, twisting, tempering, mixtures sampler_lab.benchmarks capabilities, exact-reference targets, adapters, metrics, reports, figures sampler_lab.models transparent reference targets and model-specific laboratories

Package __init__.py files define the intended import surface. Deep imports remain available for study, but callers should prefer package exports when one exists. The generated API reference renders those exports from the live docstrings and signatures.

docs.title: Protocols
docs.text:
The primary extensibility points are structural protocols rather than inheritance trees:

  • log density, differentiable log density, and twice-differentiable log density;
  • proposal and transition-kernel interfaces;
  • population and ensemble transitions;
  • continuous benchmark adapters.

A new method should implement the smallest relevant protocol, receive its RNG explicitly, and return the repository result type rather than inventing an opaque wrapper.

docs.title: Exactness labels
docs.text:
The library distinguishes:

  • exact direct or rejection sampling;
  • exact invariant kernels after Metropolis correction or frozen adaptation;
  • unbiased weighted estimators;
  • approximate variational or deterministic-particle outputs.

“Exact” never implies well mixed. “Unbiased” never implies usable variance. Result objects and benchmark reports preserve these distinctions.

docs.title: Command-line entry points
docs.text:
The supported educational CLIs are:

sampler-lab-disk-benchmark
sampler-lab-importance-demo
sampler-lab-particle-demo
sampler-lab-markov-demo
sampler-lab-ising-demo
sampler-lab-annealing-demo
sampler-lab-langevin-demo
sampler-lab-hamiltonian-demo
sampler-lab-geometry-demo
sampler-lab-policy-demo
sampler-lab-rare-event-demo
sampler-lab-benchmark

All commands expose deterministic seeds and machine-readable output where the experiment is large enough to justify downstream analysis.

docs.title: Deprecation policy
docs.text: Before 1.0, a renamed public symbol should normally remain as a documented compatibility alias for at least one minor release. Behavioral changes to RNG consumption, weight normalization, rejection-state handling, or exactness semantics require a regression test and changelog entry even when the Python signature is unchanged.

docs.title: Public References and Provenance
docs.text:
